channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: James Talarico Says ‘New Politics Is Being Born’ After Texas Primary Win
item.description: After clinching the Democratic Senate primary, he told supporters in Austin that the country’s real divide is between “top versus bottom,” not the left versus right.
item.dc:creator: Ramón Ramirez
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Talarico speaking to supporters in Austin, Texas, on Wednesday night.

item.title: Contract for El Paso ICE Center Is Under Review, Homeland Security Says
item.description: Camp East Montana in Texas has been under growing scrutiny over its living conditions and has been grappling with a measles outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Madeleine Ngo
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: Camp East Montana, a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facility at Fort Bliss in El Paso.

item.title: Daines Drops Re-election Bid in Montana, Upending a Senate Race
item.description: The surprise announcement by Senator Steve Daines could give Democrats a slim opening in the Republican-leaning state in their uphill fight to take the Senate majority.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Steve Daines said it was time for new leaders from Montana and endorsed a Republican successor.

item.title: Man Killed After Fleeing Texas Border Patrol Checkpoint, Police Say
item.description: The man fired shots at law enforcement and civilian vehicles before he was killed during a pursuit along Interstate 10 in West Texas.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.media:credit: Herika Martinez/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Border Patrol checkpoint in Sierra Blanca, Texas, last year.

item.title: Progressive Challenger Concedes in N.C.’s Bluest Congressional District
item.description: The Associated Press had not yet called the race. The contest was widely viewed as a bellwether for the type of candidate that Democratic voters wished to elevate in the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Valerie Foushee, who was first elected in 2022, faced a challenge from her left in the Democratic primary for North Carolina’s Fourth Congressional District.

item.title: Trump Follows His Gut. His National Security Advisers Try to Keep Up.
item.description: Decisions come fast, even if contradictions and inconsistencies abound. But without much of a process, there is little preparation for how things can go wrong.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald Trump in the Oval Office on Tuesday. His decision to attack Iran, he said, was based on gut instinct.

item.title: U.S. Court Takes First Steps Toward Ordering Tariff Refunds
item.description: The Trump administration is likely to appeal the move, as it ramps up its attempt to slow or potentially block the repayment of billions in past duties.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Ruth Fremson/The New York Times
item.media:description: For many importers, the liquidation process is still well underway for millions of goods that were brought ashore during President Trump’s first year back in office.

item.title: Pro-American Kurdish Forces Are Preparing Possible Iran Incursion
item.description: The C.I.A. has given small weapons to Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq in a covert program that began before the current war.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Julian E. Barnes, Christiaan Triebert, Parin Behrooz and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Safin Hamid/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A member of the Kurdistan Democratic Party of Iran inspects the damage sustained at the party’s Azadi Camp after an Iranian cross-border attack in the Kurdish region of northern Iraq.

item.title: Judge Blocks DeSantis’s Declaration of Muslim Group as Terrorist Organization
item.description: A federal judge found that the designation for CAIR, one of the nation’s largest Muslim advocacy groups, had violated the organization’s First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida signed an executive order in December declaring the Council on American-Islamic Relations a terrorist group.

item.title: Whose Airstrike Hit a Girls’ School in Iran? The U.S. Says It’s Still Investigating.
item.description: The strike was one of the deadliest attacks of the American-Israeli campaign against Iran so far.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Karoline Leavitt, the White House press secretary, was asked on Wednesday if the United States had conducted the airstrike on an Iranian school.

item.title: Noem and D.H.S. Inspector General Spar Over Obstruction Claim
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem pushed back against her department’s inspector general, who told Congress in a letter that she had “systematically obstructed” his office’s work.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian, Hamed Aleaziz and Michael Gold
item.category: Homeland Security Office of Inspector General
item.media:credit: Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images
item.media:description: The Homeland Security Department’s inspector general, Joseph V. Cuffari, testifying last year. Mr. Cuffari has opened investigations that include an audit of how Immigration and Customs Enforcement investigates use-of-force incidents.

item.title: What to Know About the Texas Runoffs in May: Key Races and Candidates
item.description: Texas voters will revisit the Republican Senate primary — and some House races where no candidate captured more than 50 percent of the vote — in runoffs on May 26.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters in line to cast their ballots at a polling site in Austin, Texas, on Tuesday.

item.title: What’s Next for Texas Under Trump? 4 Big Questions.
item.description: The biggest immediate one: What will Trump do?
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Talarico’s election night party in Austin, Texas.

item.title: Yakuza Leader Sentenced to 20 Years for Trafficking Nuclear Material
item.description: Prosecutors said Takeshi Ebisawa tried to sell uranium and plutonium from Myanmar in a plot tied to Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker
item.media:credit: United States Justice Department
item.media:description: Takeshi Ebisawa holding a rocket launcher during a meeting with undercover officers in Copenhagen in 2021.

item.title: 59% of Americans Oppose the Military Action in Iran
item.description: Three high-quality polls taken after the United States and Israel launched their attack Iran on Saturday show that a majority of Americans are not on board with the military action.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Igielnik
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A police station in Iran after U.S. and Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: Senate Thwarts Bid to Curb Trump’s War Powers on Iran
item.description: Nearly every Republican voted to block a measure that would require that President Trump win authorization from Congress to continue the offensive in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The rubble of a police station in Tehran this week after the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: James Talarico Wins the Texas Democratic Senate Race. The Real Challenge Lies Ahead.
item.description: After the state representative won the Senate primary, he joined a list of other promising progressives who have captured the left’s fancy.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: State Representative James Talarico has emerged as the Texas Democratic nominee for the Senate and is suddenly one of the hottest properties in the party.

item.title: Newsom Compares Israel to ‘Apartheid State’
item.description: The California governor, seen as a likely presidential candidate, made comments that reflect a shift in the Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Jessica Christian/San Francisco Chronicle, via Associated Press
item.media:description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom at a news conference on Monday. Mr. Newsom has for years been a firm supporter of Israel, but he has shifted his stance recently.

item.title: Aaron Spencer, Charged With Murder, Appears to Win Republican Nomination for County Sheriff in Arkansas
item.description: Aaron Spencer faces a second-degree murder charge for shooting a man accused of sexually assaulting his daughter. Preliminary results showed him appearing to win the Republican primary.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:description: A screengrab from a 2024 video by Aaron Spencer announcing his candidacy for sheriff of Lonoke County, Ark.

item.title: House Panel Votes to Subpoena Pam Bondi Over Epstein Files
item.description: The Oversight Committee took the action over the objections of its Republican chairman, after several G.O.P. members sided with Democrats to insist on it.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Attorney General Pam Bondi testifying in front of the House Judiciary Committee this month.

item.title: Infighting Begins Among Democratic Candidates for California Governor
item.description: Party leaders are starting to panic over the possibility that too many Democratic candidates could hand Republicans the governor’s office.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rusty Hicks, the California Democratic Party chairman, recently published an open letter calling on the nine most prominent candidates in his party to “honestly assess the viability” of their campaigns.

item.title: After Pete Hegseth Cut Pentagon Funding, Harvard Offers Options for Military
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has said the Pentagon would end funds supporting active-duty service members at Harvard. The school is offering military students alternatives to defer or go elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Mark Arsenault
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Kennedy, John F, School of Government
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has accused Harvard of indoctrinating students into left-leaning ideologies.

item.title: U.S. Submarine Launches Its First Torpedo in Combat Since World War II
item.description: Since 1991, Navy subs have launched scores of cruise missiles in combat, but the torpedo attack off Sri Lanka is a return to form after 80 years.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ishara S. Kodikara/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Health care workers transporting the bodies of Iranian sailors who were killed on the Iris Dena off the coast of Sri Lanka on Wednesday.

item.title: Graffiti-Covered Los Angeles Skyscrapers Find a Buyer
item.description: The windows of the unfinished Oceanwide Plaza complex in downtown Los Angeles became a canvas for street artists in 2024. A new buyer is promising to complete the project.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka
item.media:credit: Hunter Kerhart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graffiti artists covered the windows of the unfinished Oceanwide Plaza complex in downtown Los Angeles in 2024.

item.title: Gonzales Admits Affair as Republicans Move to Censure Him
item.description: The day after being forced into a runoff to keep his seat, Representative Tony Gonzales confirmed that he had an extramarital affair with an aide who later took her own life.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Tony Gonzales, Republican of Texas, could be the latest House member censured for wrongdoing.

item.title: Juan Jose Valdez, Last Marine Out of Saigon, Dies at 88
item.description: During the fall of the South Vietnamese capital in 1975, he helped evacuate thousands, and was nearly left behind.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Justice Dept., Under Pressure From Trump, Fails to Build Autopen Case Against Biden
item.description: Prosecutors in the U.S. attorney’s office in Washington were unable to build a case, underscoring the department’s increasing inability to follow through on the president’s desire to indict his rivals.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt, Devlin Barrett and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. has forcefully denied assertions that his use of the autopen broke the law, calling President Trump and his allies “liars.”

item.title: Colorado Governor Signals He’ll Commute Sentence of Election Denier
item.description: Pressured by President Trump, Gov. Jared Polis said he is considering the release of Tina Peters, the last high-profile 2020 election meddler still imprisoned. His fellow Democrats are outraged.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy, Nick Corasaniti and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Michael Ciaglo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Jared Polis of Colorado speaking at an event in Boulder last year.

item.title: Trump to Endorse in Texas Senate Republican Primary Between John Cornyn and Ken Paxton
item.description: The president said on Truth Social that he will call for the candidate who does not receive his endorsement to drop out of the race, which is heading to a runoff.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: Trader Joe’s Expands Recall of Frozen Asian Foods Over Glass Risk
item.description: No injuries had been confirmed, but consumers reported finding glass in their products. The announcement was part of a larger recall of nearly 37 million pounds of food products.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz
item.media:credit: Nick King/Lansing State Journal, via Imagn
item.media:description: Chicken fried rice packages in a refrigerator at Trader Joe’s in Meridian Township in Michigan in 2024.

item.title: Rhode Island Priests Sexually Abused Hundreds of Children, Report Finds
item.description: The report from the state’s attorney general, covering 75 years, faulted the Diocese of Providence for not removing accused priests and not contacting the police enough.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter F. Neronha, Rhode Island’s attorney general, at a news conference in December.

item.title: Interior Secretary Burgum Goes to Venezuela
item.description: The trip is part of the Trump administration’s push to build production in the South American nation.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.media:credit: Gaby Oraa/Reuters
item.media:description: The El Palito refinery of Venezuelan state oil company PDVSA in Puerto Cabello, Venezuela, earlier this year.

item.title: John Cornyn’s Biggest Challenges as Republican Senate Primary Heads for Runoff in Texas
item.description: The veteran Texas senator faces what promises to be a bruising runoff with the state attorney general challenging him from the right. But he had a stronger showing than expected.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Cornyn of Texas came out just ahead of Ken Paxton, the state’s attorney general, in the first round of primary voting on Tuesday.

item.title: U.S. Campaign in Iran Is ‘Far From Over,’ Joint Chiefs Chairman Says
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said responsibility for a strike on an Iranian girls’ school was under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People praying in front of the remains of a police station that was hit in an airstrike.

item.title: Trump Justice Dept. Seeks to Stall State Bar Discipline of Its Lawyers
item.description: The administration has no control over the disciplinary authorities of state bar associations, but a new proposal would let the attorney general ask them to suspend proceedings involving department lawyers.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Attorney General Pam Bondi during a House Judiciary Committee hearing last month.

item.title: Allies of Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders suffer primary defeats in Arkansas.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.media:credit: Maansi Srivastava for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders has made a proposed prison in central western Arkansas a key piece of her criminal justice agenda.

item.title: Al Green, Christian Menefee Head to Runoff in Texas Democratic Primary
item.description: The primary fight between the newly elected Representative Christian Menefee and the 11-term Representative Al Green was engineered by Republican redistricting that collapsed Houston districts.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Annie Mulligan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Christian Menefee, Democrat of Texas, has represented the 18th district for only a month, having won a special election for the then-vacant seat in January.

item.title: Talarico’s Win in Texas Shows That Nice Guys Can Finish First
item.description: At a moment when many Democrats are campaigning on rage and resistance, James Talarico took a different tack in his campaign in the Texas Senate primary.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: State Representative James Talarico speaking to supporters at a campaign stop in Round Rock, Texas, on Tuesday.

item.title: Democrat Nominated in Redrawn Texas House District While Counting Continues for G.O.P.
item.description: In Texas’ newly redrawn 32nd Congressional District, Democrats settled on a nominee. Republicans, facing a crowded field in a district reshaped to favor their party, awaited a final count.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: The newly redrawn 32nd District includes parts of the Dallas suburbs.

item.title: James Talarico Defeats Jasmine Crockett in Texas Senate Primary Election
item.description: State Representative James Talarico will face the winner of a Republican runoff between the incumbent, John Cornyn, and Attorney General Ken Paxton.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Emil Lippe for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Jasmine Crockett giving an update on the voting count at her watch party at Club Vivo in Dallas on Tuesday.

item.title: Chip Roy and Mayes Middleton Advance to Runoff for Texas Attorney General
item.description: No Republican reached a majority on Tuesday, but Mr. Middleton funneled millions of his own cash to secure a spot with Representative Roy,in the May 26 runoff.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times; Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Chip Roy, Republican of Texas, left, and State Senator Mayes Middleton will face off in a runoff election for the Republican nomination for Texas attorney general.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Reid J. Epstein

item.title: Iran Made Secret Outreach After Strikes, Highlighting Trump’s Challenge
item.description: President Trump is beginning to consider what sort of Iranian government might come next, as the U.S.-Israeli assault continues.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Julian E. Barnes and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Black smoke over Tehran following airstrikes on Tuesday.

item.title: On Iran, Vance Balances Between Trump and the Anti-Intervention Right
item.description: JD Vance long championed opposition to conflicts abroad. With President Trump all-in on war with Iran, the vice president is staying loyal even as allies make his qualms known.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance at the White House last month.

item.title: How RFK Jr. Is Trying to Revamp Medical School
item.description: The health secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has tapped into an old debate about how much doctors should know about nutrition. But some of his ideas, and tactics, concern medical experts.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder, Alice Callahan and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: For months, Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has been pressuring medical schools to teach more about nutrition using a variety of tactics.
